note_data.title: Measurement
keyPoints: Physics is a quantitative science — all measurements require magnitude and unit
SI System has 7 base units: metre (m), kilogram (kg), second (s), ampere (A), kelvin (K), mole (mol), candela (cd)
Length is measured using metre rule, vernier callipers, and screw gauge for different precisions
Vernier callipers can measure up to 0.1 mm accuracy, screw gauge up to 0.01 mm accuracy
Least count = Smallest division on main scale ÷ Total number of divisions on vernier scale
Mass is measured using physical balance, electronic balance, and beam balance
Time is measured using stopwatch, pendulum clock, and atomic clock
Significant figures follow specific rules — all non-zero digits are significant
Scientific notation is used to express very large or very small numbers
Accuracy refers to closeness to true value, precision refers to consistency of measurements

definitions.definition: Comparison of an unknown quantity with some known fixed quantity of the same kind called unit
A definite amount of a physical quantity taken as a standard for measurement of that quantity
The smallest measurement that can be taken accurately with a measuring instrument
An instrument used to measure internal and external diameters of tubes and thickness of sheets accurately
The number of digits in a measurement that are known with certainty plus one digit that is uncertain

importantQuestions.question: What is the least count of vernier callipers? How is it calculated?
importantQuestions.answer: Least count of vernier callipers is 0.1 mm or 0.01 cm. It is calculated as: Least count = 1 MSD - 1 VSD = 1mm - 0.9mm = 0.1mm

importantQuestions.question: Describe the construction and working of vernier callipers. How do you find the least count?
importantQuestions.answer: Vernier callipers consist of main scale and vernier scale. Main scale has fixed jaw, vernier scale has movable jaw. 10 divisions of vernier scale = 9 divisions of main scale. Reading = MSR + (VSD × LC) where MSR is main scale reading, VSD is vernier scale division that coincides with main scale. Least count = 1 MSD - 1 VSD = 0.1 mm. Used to measure external diameter, internal diameter, and depth.

mcqs.question: The least count of vernier callipers is:
Which instrument is used to measure the thickness of a coin?
The number of significant figures in 0.0025 is:

commonMistakes: Students often forget that zeros before decimal are not significant figures
Confusing least count of vernier callipers (0.1 mm) with screw gauge (0.01 mm)
Not writing proper units with final answers in numerical problems
Mixing up main scale reading with vernier scale reading in callipers
Forgetting to add MSR + (VSD × LC) formula in vernier callipers problems
examTips: This chapter usually has 2-3 MCQs and 1 long question about measuring instruments
Memorize the vernier callipers diagram and formula — appears every year
Practice significant figures rules — very common in MCQs
Learn exact definitions by heart — BSEK expects textbook language
Always show formula first, then substitute values in numerical problems
formulas: Least count of vernier callipers = 1 MSD - 1 VSD = 0.1 mm
Reading of vernier callipers = MSR + (VSD × LC)
Least count of screw gauge = Pitch ÷ Number of divisions on circular scale
note_data.keyDiagram: Labeled diagram of vernier callipers showing main scale, vernier scale, external jaws, internal jaws, and depth probe. Must show main scale reading and vernier scale coinciding line for taking readings.
